existing_plant.heat_recovery_existing: compressor jacket water → light pre-heating only; major waste heat opportunity unexploited

_golden_truth._note: Hand-calculated reference answers. Used by eval harness.
_golden_truth.baseline_carbon_assumptions: DEFRA 2026 natural gas factor 0.18293 kgCO2e/kWh; UK electricity factor 0.193 kgCO2e/kWh.

balanced_pathway_sketch.year_1: Massive waste heat recovery upgrade — chiller + compressor jacket → pre-heat all hot water (this alone displaces ~50% of HW gas demand)
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_1_2: 5 MW NH3 HP for hot water at 65°C — uses chiller waste heat as source
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_2_3: 3 MW medium-temp HP for steam pre-heat (saturated boiler feed water at 130°C)
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_3_4: 8 MW electrode boiler for steam balance + 25 MWh thermal store
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_4_plus: Phased retirement of primary gas boiler; backup retained on standby

key_decisions_for_senior_review: Waste heat recovery scale is unusually large for this site — confirm chiller load profile in summer and validate WHR capacity
5 barg steam is in HP-reachable range with high-temp HP + thermal vapour recompression — radical option worth analysing
PET blow-moulding heat demand profile dominates — agent must not double-count this against pasteurisation steam
Existing chillers are recent (age 4 and 9) — retrofit cascade-coupling is more economically attractive than chiller replacement
Sensitivity: chiller waste heat availability assumes existing chillers continue 24/7 — confirm under future production scenarios
